prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(create_entity variant_a_1)
(create_entity variant_b_1)
(create_entity aggregate_controller_1)
(create_entity aggregate_controller_2)
(reserve_resource_for_entity variant_a_1 external_resource_1)
(assign_operation_token variant_a_1 operation_token_1)
(activate_entity variant_a_1 operation_token_1)
(assign_permission_to_entity variant_a_1 permission_capability_1)
(lock_context_slot_a variant_a_1 context_slot_a_1 operation_token_1)
(prepare_variant_a_for_composition variant_a_1 context_slot_a_1 permission_capability_1)
(revoke_permission_from_entity variant_a_1 permission_capability_1)
(release_operation_token variant_a_1 operation_token_1)
(reserve_resource_for_entity variant_b_1 external_resource_2)
(assign_operation_token variant_b_1 operation_token_1)
(activate_entity variant_b_1 operation_token_1)
(assign_permission_to_entity variant_b_1 permission_capability_1)
(lock_context_slot_b variant_b_1 context_slot_b_1 operation_token_1)
(prepare_variant_b_for_composition variant_b_1 context_slot_b_1 permission_capability_1)
(revoke_permission_from_entity variant_b_1 permission_capability_1)
(release_operation_token variant_b_1 operation_token_1)
(assemble_transaction_a_marked_b_marked variant_a_1 variant_b_1 context_slot_a_1 context_slot_b_1 transaction_record_1)
(assign_operation_token variant_a_1 operation_token_1)
(progress_transaction_to_ready transaction_record_1 variant_a_1 operation_token_1)
(release_operation_token variant_a_1 operation_token_1)
(commit_variant_a variant_a_1 transaction_record_1)
(commit_variant_b variant_b_1 transaction_record_1)
(assign_operation_token variant_a_1 operation_token_1)
(finalize_entity_commit_with_audit variant_a_1 audit_entry_1 operation_token_1)
(commit_variant_a_and_release_resource variant_a_1 external_resource_1 audit_entry_1)
(release_operation_token variant_a_1 operation_token_1)
(assign_operation_token variant_b_1 operation_token_1)
(finalize_entity_commit_with_audit variant_b_1 audit_entry_1 operation_token_1)
(commit_variant_b_and_release_resource variant_b_1 external_resource_2 audit_entry_1)
(release_operation_token variant_b_1 operation_token_1)
(reserve_resource_for_entity aggregate_controller_1 external_resource_1)
(assign_operation_token aggregate_controller_1 operation_token_1)
(activate_entity aggregate_controller_1 operation_token_1)
(attach_attachment_resource_to_controller aggregate_controller_1 attachment_resource_1)
(attach_credential_to_controller aggregate_controller_1 credential_token_1)
(assign_permission_to_entity aggregate_controller_1 permission_capability_1)
(attach_configuration_item_to_transaction aggregate_controller_1 configuration_item_1 transaction_record_1)
(advance_controller_to_checkpoint_1 aggregate_controller_1 configuration_item_1 transaction_record_1 operation_token_1)
(advance_controller_to_checkpoint_2 aggregate_controller_1 attachment_resource_1 permission_capability_1 configuration_item_1 transaction_record_1)
(revoke_permission_from_entity aggregate_controller_1 permission_capability_1)
(set_controller_approval_ready aggregate_controller_1 credential_token_1 configuration_item_1 transaction_record_1)
(detach_attachment_resource_from_controller aggregate_controller_1 attachment_resource_1)
(detach_credential_from_controller aggregate_controller_1 credential_token_1)
(finalize_controller_commit_path_a aggregate_controller_1)
(finalize_entity_commit_with_audit aggregate_controller_1 audit_entry_1 operation_token_1)
(commit_controller_and_release_resource aggregate_controller_1 external_resource_1 audit_entry_1)
(release_operation_token aggregate_controller_1 operation_token_1)
(reserve_resource_for_entity aggregate_controller_2 external_resource_3)
(assign_operation_token aggregate_controller_2 operation_token_1)
(activate_entity aggregate_controller_2 operation_token_1)
(attach_attachment_resource_to_controller aggregate_controller_2 attachment_resource_1)
(attach_credential_to_controller aggregate_controller_2 credential_token_1)
(assign_permission_to_entity aggregate_controller_2 permission_capability_1)
(attach_configuration_item_to_transaction aggregate_controller_2 configuration_item_2 transaction_record_1)
(advance_controller_to_checkpoint_1 aggregate_controller_2 configuration_item_2 transaction_record_1 operation_token_1)
(advance_controller_to_checkpoint_2 aggregate_controller_2 attachment_resource_1 permission_capability_1 configuration_item_2 transaction_record_1)
(revoke_permission_from_entity aggregate_controller_2 permission_capability_1)
(set_controller_approval_ready aggregate_controller_2 credential_token_1 configuration_item_2 transaction_record_1)
(detach_attachment_resource_from_controller aggregate_controller_2 attachment_resource_1)
(detach_credential_from_controller aggregate_controller_2 credential_token_1)
(finalize_controller_commit_path_a aggregate_controller_2)
(finalize_entity_commit_with_audit aggregate_controller_2 audit_entry_1 operation_token_1)
(commit_controller_and_release_resource aggregate_controller_2 external_resource_3 audit_entry_1)
(release_operation_token aggregate_controller_2 operation_token_1)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem is complex with many entities and actions. The goal is to commit four entities: variant_a_1, variant_b_1, aggregate_controller_1, and aggregate_controller_2.


## Understanding the problem  
- The task involves committing four specific entities: variant_a_1, variant_b_1, aggregate_controller_1, and aggregate_controller_2.  
- The problem appears complex, with multiple entities and actions that need careful handling.  
- The goal is to ensure all four entities are processed correctly as part of the commitment process.

## Assessing Available Resources  
- Found external resources (external_resource_1,2,3) and limited tokens like operation_token_1 and permission_capability_1.  
- Identified single instances of attribute_value_1, credential_token_1, and attachment_resource_1, with multiple entities needing them.  

## Examining Entity Creation  
- Need to create four domain entities: variant_a_1, variant_b_1, aggregate_controller_1, and aggregate_controller_2 before committing.  
- Entities currently uncreated, with links like variant_a_1 to context_slot_a_1 already defined.  

## Planning Entity Commitment  
- To commit, each entity requires creation, resource reservation, and activation, using limited resources like the single operation_token_1.  
- Process involves locking context slots and either preparing for composition or attaching attributes, with permissions needed for multiple steps.  

## Analyzing Resource Constraints  
- Only three external resources available for four entities, requiring careful allocation.  
- Tokens and permissions can be released after use, suggesting potential reuse to manage scarcity.

## Exploring Variant Commitment  
- For variant_a_1, the process starts with creation, resource reservation (e.g., external_resource_1), and activation, requiring op_token_1.  
- A simple path to entity_ready involves locking context_slot_a, preparing for composition, and committing, ensuring variant_locked and variant_ready states.  
- Assembling a transaction needs both variants locked, contexts locked, and ready, progressing to transaction_ready for further commitment.  
- Committing variant_a involves setting entity_ready, finalizing with audit, and releasing resources, making it fully committed.  
- The approach allows reusing tokens and permissions, aligning with resource constraints.

## Investigating Controller Commitment  
- Controllers like aggregate_controller_1 can reach entity_ready via a short path, independent of transactions or variants, using finalize stage2.  
- Reference markers for controllers are limited; aggregate_controller_1 uses reference_marker_1, but reference_marker_2 is unavailable for aggregate_controller_2 initially.

## Sequencing Entity Creation  
- Create variant_a_1 and variant_b_1 first, as they require resources early for reservation and activation, using operation_token_1 sequentially.  
- Controllers like aggregate_controller_1 can be created later, as their resource needs may align after variants are processed.  

## Managing Resource Allocation  
- Assign three external resources to variant_a_1, variant_b_1, and one controller initially, planning to reuse resources later due to the scarcity of four entities needing only three resources.  
- Release operation_token_1 after locking context for variant_a_1 to make it available for variant_b_1, ensuring sequential processing fits the single token constraint.  

## Handling Permission Flow  
- Use permission_capability_1 for preparing variant_a_1 for composition after activation, then revoke and reassign it to variant_b_1 to manage the single permission effectively.  
- Ensure both variants are locked and ready, with contexts locked, before assembling the transaction, requiring careful sequencing of permission and token use.  

## Assembling the Transaction  
- Assemble transaction after both variants are locked and ready, linking to context_slot_a_1 and context_slot_b_1, ensuring no alternates and transaction availability.  
- Reassign operation_token_1 to variant_a_1 to progress transaction to ready, enabling further commitment steps like committing variant_a_1 to entity_ready.

## Detailing Resource Sequencing  
- Create all entities (variant_a_1, variant_b_1, aggregate_controller_1, aggregate_controller_2) first, as creation has no prerequisites beyond not being committed or created.  
- Start with variant_a_1: reserve resource_1, assign operation_token_1, activate, lock context, and prepare for composition, ensuring sequential resource use.  

## Managing Token and Permission Reuse  
- Release operation_token_1 and permission_capability_1 after locking variant_a_1 to make them available for variant_b_1, fitting the single token constraint.  
- Follow the same process for variant_b_1, revoking and releasing resources after preparation to prepare for transaction assembly.  

## Advancing Transaction Readiness  
- Assemble the transaction after both variants are locked and ready, linking to context slots, but it remains reserved, not ready.  
- Reassign operation_token_1 to variant_a_1 to progress the transaction to ready, enabling further commitment steps.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
